title: Memorializing or Closing Social Media Accounts
summary: Most major platforms offer a choice between memorializing an account or closing it entirely — understanding the difference helps you decide.
category: After a Loss III: Estate & Digital
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Memorializing or Closing Social Media Accounts | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Most major platforms offer a choice between memorializing an account or closing it entirely — understanding the difference helps you decide.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Memorialized accounts typically remain visible but are marked and frozen.
Closing an account permanently deletes it and its content.
Requirements to prove death and relationship vary by platform.

paragraphs: Memorializing an account (an option on platforms like Facebook and Instagram) generally keeps the profile visible for friends and family to see and share memories, while preventing login or further changes — a middle path many families prefer.

Fully closing an account typically requires more documentation (a death certificate, proof of relationship, sometimes a court order) and permanently removes the content, which is worth thinking through since it usually can't be undone.

nextSteps: Decide whether memorializing or closing better fits the family's wishes for each platform.
Gather the documentation each platform requires.
Consider saving photos or content before closing an account, since it's permanent.
disclaimer: General guidance — each platform's specific process and requirements are subject to change; check their current policy directly.
